description: IPO count rank correlation with CAPE
interpretation: Correlation near zero means IPO count alone is not a reliable valuation-greed gauge.

description: IPO count rank correlation with forward P/E
interpretation: Correlation near zero means IPO count alone is not a reliable valuation-greed gauge.

description: IPO count rank correlation with trailing P/E
interpretation: Correlation near zero means IPO count alone is not a reliable valuation-greed gauge.

description: Years in top quartile of IPO count, 1960-2025
interpretation: The high-IPO-count sample is large enough to inspect across regimes.

description: Top-quartile IPO count years that also had top-quartile CAPE
interpretation: Only 3 of 17 high-IPO-count years were also high-CAPE years: 1997, 1999 and 2000.

description: Top-quartile IPO count years without top-quartile CAPE
interpretation: Most high-count IPO years did not occur at extreme broad-market valuation.

description: Top-quartile CAPE years without top-quartile IPO count
interpretation: High valuation can persist even when operating-company IPO counts are low.

description: Mean S&P 500 forward 1y return after point-in-time high IPO-count years minus other years
interpretation: Uses expanding IPO-count percentiles to avoid lookahead; not a mechanical sell rule.

description: Mean S&P 500 forward 3y return after point-in-time high IPO-count years minus other years
interpretation: Uses expanding IPO-count percentiles to avoid lookahead; not a mechanical sell rule.

description: Mean S&P 500 forward 5y return after point-in-time high IPO-count years minus other years
interpretation: Uses expanding IPO-count percentiles to avoid lookahead; not a mechanical sell rule.
